name: Rokid GMaps
summary: Turn-by-turn navigation for Rokid AR glasses.
description: Phone plus glasses navigation workflow with a HUD for directions. Install both APKs for the full experience.
author: Anezium

listing.descriptionMarkdown:
## About
Rokid GMaps is a navigation app designed for Rokid AR glasses paired with a phone. It offers turn-by-turn directions displayed on a HUD in the glasses, while the phone handles search and route calculation.

## Key Features
- Phone app manages UI, place search, and route calculation
- Glasses app displays navigation HUD
- Supports Google Places and Routes APIs as well as OSM/Nominatim/OSRM fallback
- Provider selection configurable in phone app settings

## How It Works
- The phone app calculates routes and streams navigation data to the glasses via Bluetooth
- The glasses app renders directions using the original Rokid-Maps architecture

## Requirements
- Both phone and glasses APKs must be installed
- Optional Google API key setup for enhanced search and routing
- Bluetooth connection between phone and glasses

## Notes
- Google API usage requires enabling billing and APIs in Google Cloud
- Google provider features are optional and disabled by default

releases.notes: Migration release for the new com.anezium.rokidgmaps package IDs. Includes both phone and glasses debug APKs so RokidBrew can update the paired app entry.

Phone app only.

- Fix Android splash screen branding with the real Rokid-GMaps logo asset.
- Add a dedicated padded splash asset and Android 12+ splash theme wiring.
- Keep existing launcher icons unchanged.

## Highlights

- First MVP release of `Rokid-GMaps` for phone + Rokid AR glasses navigation.
- Optional Google Places API v1 and Routes API v2 support, with OSM/OSRM fallback kept as the default base.
- Drive, Walk, and Transit modes with phone-side route preview and a live AR HUD on the glasses.
- Nearby search shortcuts, notification forwarding, Wi-Fi credential sharing, and OTA APK push support.

## Included In This Release

- Added project documentation with architecture, setup steps, limitations, and upstream credits.
- Added a versioned `CHANGELOG.md` for release tracking.
- Updated Android launcher icons for both apps.
- Switched both manifests to `@mipmap/ic_launcher` and packaged density-specific launcher assets.

## Verification

- Verified with `./gradlew assembleDebug` on 2026-03-27.
- Debug APKs attached when available:
  - `phone/build/outputs/apk/debug/phone-debug.apk`
  - `glasses/build/outputs/apk/debug/glasses-debug.apk`
